what_it_does: RG10 full-scale runner: an incremental, resume-safe batch wrapper around registry_generation_run.run_registry_generation. It loads candidates.json, derives the set of mockups, adopts any mockup whose batch artifact already exists (with n_dossiers) as done, then for each not-yet-done, not-permanently-failed mockup within a time budget runs the full RG3-RG6 chain (GROUND -> MAP -> REDUCE cluster-dedup -> CONFIRM), writes the per-mockup artifact rg10-batch-<stem>.json, and records done/failed state in .build/rg10/state.json. Fail-loud: a chain that raises OR yields 0 dossiers from >0 candidates is recorded in state['failed'] with a reason and retried up to MAX_ATTEMPTS=2, never marked done. Proposes only — artifacts surface for Tim's approve; nothing writes addresses.json or the mockups.
observations: Reuses the proven registry_generation_run module rather than parallelizing — 'reuse-don't-parallel' is stated explicitly, citing the three manual seams it already supplies.
Docstring cites proven-by-use numbers on C1-inbox-desktop: 43 candidates -> 43 dossiers -> 28 clusters -> 14 confirmed / 14 flagged.
_adopt_existing brings stranded/prior artifacts into 'done' so incomplete work is in-scope and not re-burned.
FAIL-LOUD lesson documented: the first draft read only .resolved and silently swallowed 940 .failed units; this version records failures explicitly.
MAX_ATTEMPTS=2 ('blocked-twice discipline'): retried once, then left loud in state for the surface step rather than retried forever.
State write is atomic-ish via _save after each mockup; resume-safe across bounded batches.
The per-mockup artifact shape matches what registry_generation_run's __main__ writes — 'one canonical shape'.
Time budget default 420s, overridable via argv.
Returns a rollup: ran_this_batch, mockups_done/total, failed, confirmed_total, flagged_total.
standouts: The fail-loud-on-zero-dossiers rule (line 89) directly encodes a remembered prior bug where silent .failed swallowing hid 940 lost units.
Adoption of pre-existing artifacts makes the runner idempotent and respectful of prior compute.
Bounded time budget + per-mockup state = composable batches that drive the whole 23-mockup / 940-candidate set to completion across runs.
conventions: Family pattern: os.chdir to repo root, .build/rg10 state/artifacts, 'THE FLOOR: this run PROPOSES' framing, nothing writes the live registry.
Resume-safe state file with done/failed dicts and setdefault guards.
Reuse of registry_generation_run as the single source of the generation chain.
concerns: A permanently-failed mockup (attempts >= MAX_ATTEMPTS) is silently skipped on subsequent runs except for its presence in returned 'failed' — relies entirely on the surface step to notice it.
_adopt_existing only checks d.get('n_dossiers') truthiness; an artifact with n_dossiers>0 but a degenerate confirmed/flagged split would still be adopted as done.
No de-dup across mockups happens here (that is rg10_reconcile's job), so 'confirmed_total' here counts per-mockup confirmations, which the docstring of reconcile shows is inflated.
notes: RG10 batch driver: resume-safe wrapper that runs the proven per-mockup generation chain across all mockups, adopts existing artifacts, fails loud on zero-dossier runs, and proposes per-mockup artifacts without touching the live registry.
questions: What exactly does run_registry_generation return beyond the keys read here, and is the artifact shape guaranteed stable with its __main__?
How does the surface step consume state['failed'] to flag permanently-failed mockups to Tim?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — docstring describes a resume-safe incremental wrapper that runs the full chain per mockup, fails loud, and proposes artifacts, which is precisely the implementation.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud over silent swallowing (the remembered .failed bug)
Adopt stranded work rather than re-burn it
Propose-only; the live registry write stays behind approval
intention_signals: Scale a proven single-mockup generation chain to the full corpus safely and incrementally, ensuring no unit is silently lost and the human only ever approves proposed artifacts.
novelty: The explicit codification of a past silent-failure bug into a fail-loud invariant (zero-dossier-from-nonzero-candidates is a recorded failure, not a done), plus the blocked-twice retry discipline.
contribution: Contributes the corpus-scale orchestration entry point of the RG10 pipeline: it produces the per-mockup batch artifacts that reconcile and the repair passes downstream all consume.
summary_for_embedding: A resume-safe incremental batch runner that wraps registry_generation_run to execute the full per-mockup generation chain across all mockups, adopts existing artifacts, fails loud on zero-dossier runs, and proposes per-mockup batch artifacts.
intention_for_embedding: To scale the proven single-mockup registry-generation chain to the whole corpus incrementally and safely, never silently dropping units and never writing the live registry without approval.
